description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The panels are elevated above a patterned platform, which appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, possibly for structural support and maintenance access. The structure includes several support posts and braces, ensuring stability and durability in outdoor environments. On one side, there are visible control or electrical boxes, likely housing power electronics such as inverters or junction boxes. The platform beneath the panels is marked with distinct geometric patterns and colored sections, possibly for safety or operational zoning. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for efficient solar energy capture and integration into urban or semi-urban settings.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a sturdy metal frame with a patterned support platform and visible electrical components. Designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban environments.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the solar panel array from an elevated angle, highlighting the grid of panels and the supporting frame structure beneath.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side profile, clearly displaying the height of the frame, the patterned platform, and the electrical box on the right.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a rear perspective, emphasizing the depth of the platform, the support posts, and the colored safety zone at the base.
description_view_3: View 3 provides a side view from the opposite direction, showing the length of the platform, the angled supports, and the arrangement of the panels above.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with steel posts and aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of approximately 8 panels arranged side by side
